channel.title: Working with Designs on Layer5 Documentation
channel.description: Recent content in Working with Designs on Layer5 Documentation

item.title: Understanding Relationships
item.description:
What are Relationships? 🔗

Relationships define the nature of interaction between interconnected components in Kanvas. They represent various types of connections and dependencies between components no matter the genealogy of the relationship such as parent, siblings, binding.

Types of Relationships 🔗

Relationships are categorized into different kinds, types, and subtypes, so that can be expressive of the specific manner in which one or more components relate to one another.

item.title: Helm Kanvas Snapshot
item.description: The Kanvas Snapshot Helm Plugin allows users to generate a visual snapshot of their Helm charts directly from the command line. It simplifies the process of creating Meshery Snapshots, providing a visual representation of packaged Helm charts. This plugin integrates with Meshery Cloud and GitHub Actions to automate the workflow of snapshot creation, which is especially useful for Helm users who need to quickly visualize their chart configurations.

item.title: Kanvas GitHub Snapshot
item.description:
Kanvas Snapshot is a screenshot service provided via Kanvas for your designs. Visualize changes to your code-base with each pull request made. The SnapShot GitHub Action is configurable from within your Cloud account.

How the SnapShot service works

Functional Sequence Diagram

Installing Kanvas Snapshot: Github Pull Request

Connect MeshMap to your GitHub repo and see changes pull request-to-pull request. Get snapshots of your infrastructure directly in your PRs.
